Implement exponential backoff retry mechanism with configurable delays (start at 1s, max 60s). 3. Create fallback data source integration (Alpha Vantage, IEX Cloud, or Polygon.io) with automatic switching when Yahoo Finance fails. 5. Add comprehensive error handling and logging to track API health and usage patterns. 6. Implement data caching mechanism to reduce API calls for recently fetched data. 7. Add configuration options for API timeout settings, retry attempts, and rate limits. 8. Create data validation layer to ensure fetched financial data integrity before DCF calculations.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Create unit tests for rate limiting logic with mock API responses returning 429 errors. 2. Test exponential backoff behavior with simulated network delays and API failures. 3. Monitor API usage patterns in test environment to confirm rate limiting effectiveness. 8. Test error recovery scenarios including network timeouts and malformed responses.

Create a prioritized update plan categorizing dependencies by risk level: critical security updates (immediate), major version updates (careful testing), and minor updates (low risk). 4. Update dependencies incrementally, starting with security patches and minor updates, then testing thoroughly before proceeding to major version updates. 5. Update import statements and API calls where breaking changes exist, particularly for data processing libraries like pandas, numpy, or financial APIs. 6. Verify all existing functionality still works after each dependency update by running the full test suite and manual validation of core features like DCF calculations and data fetching.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Run comprehensive test suite after each dependency update to catch breaking changes early. 2. Test all major workflows: data fetching from Yahoo Finance, DCF calculations, Excel export functionality, and Streamlit UI components. 3. Test with sample financial data to ensure calculations remain accurate after library updates. 5. Check for any new deprecation warnings or errors in logs that might indicate future compatibility issues. 6. tasks.title: Optimize Requirements Installation for Fast Subsequent Builds
tasks.description: Optimize requirements.txt installation to reduce subsequent build times from 2-3 minutes to 5-15 seconds while keeping first install comprehensive with all features.

Implement smart dependency checking instead of forced reinstallation with intelligent logic to only install if packages are missing or requirements.txt changed. 3. Optimize pip flags for better performance by adding --prefer-binary and --only-binary=:all: for faster wheel installations, and preserve pip cache between runs. 4. Pin exact versions for heavy dependencies (kaleido ~50-100MB, scipy with compiled extensions, streamlit ecosystem with 50+ dependencies) to enable perfect cache hits on subsequent installs. 5. Reorder dependencies in requirements.txt for optimal pip resolution by placing heavy, stable dependencies first (numpy, scipy) and grouping related packages together. 6. Add virtual environment creation/reuse logic to batch script with environment validation and reset option for troubleshooting.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Test across different scenarios: fresh installation, requirements.txt changes, cache clearing, and dependency conflicts. 5. Validate that optimized installation maintains compatibility with all existing features including PDF generation, chart exports, and data analysis capabilities.

tasks.title: Fix FCF Calculation Producing Near-Zero Values for DCF Analysis
tasks.description: The Free Cash Flow calculations are producing essentially zero values (~$95K for Microsoft instead of tens of billions), which makes DCF valuations meaningless. tasks.details: PROBLEM: FCF values show as ~$0.1M per year instead of $20-50B per year for Microsoft. ROOT CAUSE: Issue is in financial data extraction/processing upstream from DCF calculation. IMPACT: DCF Enterprise Values show as $3.5M instead of $3.5T. IMPLEMENTATION PLAN: 1. Analyze Excel data structure in MSFT folder to understand data format and units, 2. Debug financial metrics extraction in _calculate_all_metrics() function, 3. Investigate Excel parsing logic in _load_excel_data() and related functions, 4. Add logging to trace values from Excel files through to FCF calculations, 5. Fix root cause (likely Excel column/row parsing, unit scaling, or field name matching), 6. Validate fix produces realistic FCF values in billions range for large companies.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with MSFT data to ensure FCF values are reasonable (tens of billions). Verify DCF calculation produces realistic enterprise values (trillions for MSFT). tasks.details: Implement data quality metrics system: 1) Create DataQualityAnalyzer class in core/data_processing/ to assess data completeness, accuracy, consistency, and timeliness across all financial data sources, 2) Implement quality scoring algorithms that evaluate missing data percentage, data age/freshness, source reliability, calculation confidence intervals, and cross-validation between data sources, 3) Add quality indicators to all financial calculations showing confidence levels (high/medium/low) with detailed explanations, 4) Create data quality dashboard in Streamlit interface displaying real-time quality metrics, data source health status, and recommendations for improving data reliability, 5) Implement quality-based warnings system that alerts users to potential data issues and their impact on valuation accuracy, 6) Add data lineage tracking to show data source paths and transformation history, 7) Integrate quality metrics into existing caching system with quality-based cache invalidation
tasks.testStrategy: Test data quality calculations across various data completeness scenarios (0%, 50%, 100% complete datasets). Verify quality scoring algorithms produce consistent and meaningful reliability indicators. Test integration with all existing financial calculation engines (FCF, DCF, DDM, P/B). Validate quality indicators display correctly in Streamlit interface with proper color coding and explanations. Test data source health monitoring detects API failures and stale data. Verify quality-based cache invalidation works correctly when data quality changes.

tasks.title: Extend FinancialCalculationEngine with Liquidity Ratios
tasks.description: Add comprehensive liquidity ratio calculations to the existing FinancialCalculationEngine class
tasks.details: Implement calculate_current_ratio(), calculate_quick_ratio(), calculate_cash_ratio(), and calculate_working_capital() methods in core/analysis/engines/financial_calculation_engine.py. Use existing CalculationResult pattern and validation approaches. Formulas: Current Ratio = Current Assets / Current Liabilities, Quick Ratio = (Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities, Cash Ratio = (Cash + Cash Equivalents) / Current Liabilities, Working Capital = Current Assets - Current Liabilities. Include comprehensive input validation, error handling, and metadata tracking consistent with existing engine methods.
tasks.testStrategy: Create unit tests for each ratio method with edge cases, invalid inputs, and mathematical validation against known benchmarks

subtasks.title: Implement calculate_current_ratio method
subtasks.description: Add current ratio calculation method to FinancialCalculationEngine following existing patterns and validation
subtasks.details: Create calculate_current_ratio() method that takes current_assets and current_liabilities as parameters. Formula: Current Ratio = Current Assets / Current Liabilities. Use existing CalculationResult pattern with comprehensive input validation, handle zero/negative denominators, and include metadata tracking. Follow the same structure as existing methods like calculate_pb_ratio with proper error handling and logging.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with edge cases including zero current liabilities, negative values, None inputs, and validation against expected current ratio benchmarks (>2.0 strong, 1.0-2.0 adequate, <1.0 weak)

subtasks.title: Implement calculate_quick_ratio method
subtasks.description: Add quick ratio (acid-test ratio) calculation method excluding inventory from current assets
subtasks.details: Create calculate_quick_ratio() method that takes current_assets, inventory, and current_liabilities as parameters. Formula: Quick Ratio = (Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities. Include comprehensive input validation, handle cases where inventory exceeds current assets, and provide warnings for unusual values. Use the established CalculationResult pattern with proper metadata including all input values and calculation method.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests covering scenarios where inventory equals or exceeds current assets, zero inventory cases, negative inventory values, and mathematical validation against known financial benchmarks (>1.0 strong, 0.5-1.0 moderate, <0.5 weak)

subtasks.title: Implement calculate_cash_ratio method
subtasks.description: Add cash ratio calculation method for the most conservative liquidity measure
subtasks.details: Create calculate_cash_ratio() method that takes cash_and_equivalents and current_liabilities as parameters. Formula: Cash Ratio = (Cash + Cash Equivalents) / Current Liabilities. Include validation for reasonable cash levels, handle zero cash scenarios, and log warnings for extremely high or low cash ratios. Follow existing error handling patterns and provide detailed metadata about cash position relative to liabilities.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with scenarios including zero cash, excessive cash positions (>50% of current liabilities), negative cash values, and validation against typical cash ratio ranges (>0.2 strong, 0.1-0.2 adequate, <0.1 weak)

subtasks.title: Implement calculate_working_capital method
subtasks.description: Add working capital calculation method for absolute liquidity measurement
subtasks.details: Create calculate_working_capital() method that takes current_assets and current_liabilities as parameters. Formula: Working Capital = Current Assets - Current Liabilities. Handle negative working capital scenarios with appropriate warnings, validate input ranges, and include metadata about working capital adequacy. Provide contextual information about positive vs negative working capital implications for business operations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests covering positive working capital (healthy operations), negative working capital (potential liquidity issues), zero working capital edge case, and very large working capital values. Include validation against industry standards and business size considerations
